Great Hearts, Noble Hearts
gameplay / duels
Jules Lavelle 8/11/2020 12:16 PM
Duels cannot be fought for just any reason. But if a reason and opportunity are present, a character who fails to fight will lose status points equal to half his social level. Whether cause for a duel is valid is decided by all non-affiliated players. In order to find no cause, there must be a unanimous vote among these players. Examples of cause: * Two characters of unfriendly regiments meet. * Committing an indiscretion with another player's mistress. * Insulting a regimental friend of a character. * Two characters courting the same mistress. * A noble meeting a non-noble who is 4 or more social levels above him. (edited)
Jules Lavelle 8/11/2020 12:26 PM
=================== Duel Structure: Duels are divided into turns. Players submit orders for the duel by selecting routines. Each routine consists of one or more actions. Each action takes one turn to perform. Players are limited to actions that are part of the routines listed under Dueling Table A; actions cannot be submitted that are not part of a full routine. Duels proceed in 12-turn blocks. The player with higher Expertise has the advantage, and plots his first set of routines at least 6 turns at a time, then 12 turns after that. The player with lower Expertise must plot at least 12 turns at a time. Each turn, the players' actions are compared and the results evaluated based on Dueling Table B. The duel continues in this manner until one player surrenders or is killed. (edited)
Jules Lavelle 8/11/2020 12:45 PM
=================== Optional Routines: Although routines must be followed to completion once begun, on any turn following a completed routine, players may cancel into an optional routine instead of beginning the next planned routine. These optional routines are limited to Optional Block, Optional Parry, Riposte, and Surrender. Riposte is not its own routine, but an optional action that may be performed after successfully parrying an opponent's lunge. An optional routine will completely cancel the routine it replaces. Any remaining actions that were plotted as part of that routine will be converted to rests. Surrender may be performed at any turn, even in the middle of another routine. =================== Optional Weapons: Players may bring whatever weapon they wish to a duel, as in Dueling Table C. Weapons can be broken whenever an attack is blocked or parried. Broken weapons count as daggers. Dagger wielders must precede all attacks with a Close routine. Against a foe wielding a 2-hander, he must precede with 2 Close routines before attacking. Players may throw their weapons. For weapons other than daggers, cutlasses or 2-handers, the throw is a success on a 1d6 roll of 1 or 2, and the target takes damage. Thrown daggers hit on a 1, 2, or 3. Thrown cutlasses and 2-handers hit only on a result of 2 when rolling 2d6, but kill instantly. Players who have thrown their weapons are unarmed and can no longer damage an armed opponent, and are limited to the Rest, Jump Back, and Surrender routines. If both players have thrown their weapons, they can use the Optional Recover routine, consisting of actions -OR1-OR2-. The first one to complete this routine may choose which weapon to recover, but cannot take both. (edited)
12:47 PM
=================== Wounds: Players whose Endurance drops below half its maximum must add one mandatory rest routine into each 12-turn sequence. Players whose Endurance drops to zero or below are dead. In the event a player survives a duel, they recovery one half of the Endurance points lost the first week after that duel. Each week thereafter, Endurance equal to the player's Constitution is recovered. A player with less than 50% of Endurance may decline a duel, regardless of cause, without loss of status. But accepting a duel in such a state instantly confers 3 status points.

Narrator 8/15/2020 7:38 PM
@harry had his sights set on the more respectable of the Demachy twins, Alida. He had everything in order -- boots shined, shirt freshly laundered, and a gorgeous bouquet tucked under his arm -- as he made his way up the avenue towards her apartments. Much to his shock, he found none other before her door than @Ghôst Touchard, similarly appointed for courting. The two men stared at one another with suspicion. Their intentions were as obvious as they were mutually exclusive. Alida, standing in the doorway, clutched her hands to her breast, awaiting to learn which man's love was strongest. Her sister interrupted preparations for her own courting to stare down from her own window, and a crowd begin to gather around the men.
7:39 PM
==== Does anyone dispute that this is cause for a duel?
4
Blastagarded 8/15/2020 7:47 PM
This is not some Court of Solomon. The lady's heart cannot be divided. It can only be won through a test of courage.

As newcomers and friends they arrived, but have thusly discovered they yern for the same fair maiden! Let them settle this with a duel, and let us all see who is the quality of man who will stand and deliver! Let them duel!

Narrator 8/16/2020 12:07 PM
Renowned far and wide for her beauty (and no less for her family's connections), Adélaïde Blaise has long been one of the most sought-after young women in Paris. @Jean Renault had this in mind as he arrived at her doorstep to make clear his intentions and win her heart. But as he raised his hand to the knocker, he felt a tap at his shoulder. @laiv.wire stood behind, well-bred eyes narrowed at Leon's impertinence. Young Adélaïde peered out from the window, and the men knew they were at an impasse. A crowd began to form.
harry 8/16/2020 12:14 PM
These men of status clearly seek the same heart! Let them enter formal combat!
Jules Lavelle 8/16/2020 12:15 PM
May their blades be as stout as their hearts.
laiv.wire 8/16/2020 12:32 PM
@Jean Renault I bear you no ill will for seeking the company of a lady so fair, but her magnificence implores that I challenge you for her hand. Draw, good sir. (edited)

laiv.wire 8/16/2020 1:55 PM

Jules Lavelle 8/16/2020 1:56 PM
My, a true gentleman. Noble blood shows, after all.
Jean Renault 8/16/2020 2:03 PM
Alas, I am not born of the nobility, but I aspire to reach the heights of the true essence of such
Ghôst Touchard 8/16/2020 2:03 PM
You are one of the luckiest men I know. Unfortunately, bad luck is luck.
1
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 1:54 PM
Does riposte wipe out the next planned routine, or is it inserted before then?
Narrator 8/22/2020 2:00 PM
the R is an optional action on the back end of the parry routine. if you successfully parry, you have the option of replacing the next action with the R
2:01 PM
if that action is part of a longer routine, it will cancel the entire routine
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 2:01 PM
Gotcha
Narrator 8/22/2020 2:01 PM
a cancelled routine just reverts to rests for the duration of that routine
2:02 PM
if you're disadvantaged such that you need to add extra rests, after a parry is a great place to put them!
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 2:04 PM
So if you're less than 50% END, you need to add in a rest, if it's the rest after a parry and you override it with riposte, do you need to add an extra rest, or does that comply?
Jules Lavelle 8/22/2020 2:12 PM
That complies. The requirements are just what you plan, not what you execute
Blastagarded 8/22/2020 3:24 PM
Jules Lavelle 8/22/2020 3:25 PM
believe in the heart of the routines
Narrator 8/22/2020 3:37 PM
To set the stage for the duel. Pierre has advantage, high enough that Jean-Luc must add one mandatory rest routine into each sequence. When you've finished peacocking about in the club chat, name your weapons.
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 3:39 PM
Is a sequence 12 actions or 6? I know I have to provide 12+, but for rest purposes
Narrator 8/22/2020 3:39 PM
12
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 3:40 PM
I draw my sabre and shout 'Glory to the Archduke Leopold's Cuirassiers!' (edited)
Jules Lavelle 8/22/2020 5:20 PM
@Ghôst Touchard ready soon?
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 5:21 PM
Sure thing- should I submit orders or wait?
Jules Lavelle 8/22/2020 5:21 PM
You can submit your first sequence
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 5:31 PM
So if Iose 50% END, would I need to rest twice per sequence?
Abelard Soucy 8/22/2020 5:32 PM
I also draw my sabre as I prepare to defend the Grand Duke Max's Dragroons!
Jules Lavelle 8/22/2020 5:33 PM
yes, being heavily wounded adds 1 extra, just as being heavily disadvantaged does
Careless Ocelot 8/22/2020 5:34 PM

Abelard Soucy 8/22/2020 5:52 PM

Narrator 8/22/2020 5:53 PM

harry 8/22/2020 5:53 PM

Abelard Soucy 8/22/2020 5:56 PM

5:56 PM

Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 5:57 PM
I should hope I still have enough vigor and humours for the mlle Demachy!
1
Abelard Soucy 8/22/2020 5:57 PM

5:57 PM

Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 5:57 PM

harry 8/22/2020 5:58 PM

harry 8/22/2020 5:59 PM

6:00 PM

Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 6:01 PM

Blastagarded 8/28/2020 11:45 PM
Dare I say, a potential duel is in the making between @Jean Renault and @laiv.wire. Should it come to pass, it would be ungentlemanly of me to wish my future guest ill will... I've got 35 crowns on my man @Jean Renault to the first neutral party interested in the wager.
Ghôst Touchard 8/28/2020 11:46 PM
I said I would refrain from gambling this month, but I cannot resist a good bet. I shall take you up on that, should the duel come to pass!

Jean Renault 8/28/2020 11:48 PM
@laiv.wire The fates are truly against us these days!
Blastagarded 8/29/2020 12:30 AM
Alright @Jean Renault, I know its only the first week of May, but for some reason I have this terrible feeling some bad news is coming my way. So if you would kindly kick his ass, I would greatly appreciated it.
12:31 AM
My apologizes. My coarse upbringing is showing through.
laiv.wire 8/29/2020 3:17 AM
sigh This is truly becoming very vexing, Leon. I don't wish to fight again, but I have little other choice. My life grows colder without a companion.
Careless Ocelot 8/29/2020 8:30 AM
Ah the monthly duel. checks the sun Honestly I'm not sure what I'd do without such a regular spectacle to attend.
Ghôst Touchard 8/29/2020 10:38 AM

Blastagarded 8/29/2020 10:50 AM

Anonymouse 10/12/2020 10:33 PM

Curtis Sinnoch 10/12/2020 10:33 PM
I shall fight with the dagger! This once erstwhile friend has been brought up while attending gatherings with me, and thus, I shall favor the weapon he and his father likely did before joining the Elite.
Narrator 10/12/2020 10:38 PM
Alphonse has the advantage, and will submit at least 6 turns' worth of routines to start. Curtis will submit at least 12 turns' worth. (edited)
Curtis Sinnoch 10/12/2020 10:44 PM

Narrator 10/12/2020 10:46 PM

Anonymouse 10/12/2020 10:48 PM

Lucien dual-wields pidgeons ready for battle

Curtis Sinnoch 10/26/2020 9:25 AM
Curtis brandishes his former regimental weapon, a Sabre
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 9:27 AM
Lucien produces his rapier
Narrator 10/26/2020 9:44 AM

Turn One

Narrator 10/26/2020 9:46 AM
The gentlemen cross blades as Lucien leaps forward, a dozen coruscating doves forming a slow-motion halo in the background. Curtis meets him in the middle with a vicious strike, but each man's blade finds a home in the flesh of the other. (edited)
1
9:47 AM
Curtis takes 12 damage. Lucien takes 30 damage.
Jules Lavelle 10/26/2020 9:48 AM
Show him what for, Sinnoch! \hoots** (edited)

I REST.

Narrator 10/26/2020 9:50 AM
Curtis sees his chance and drives his blade down as though he were astride his charger. There is a sickening crunch as Lucien's skeleton bears the brunt of the blow, and splinters of clavicle spatter the aghast audience.
9:50 AM
Lucien takes 60 damage.
Blastagarded 10/26/2020 9:51 AM
Less a duel than an execution it seems.
Narrator 10/26/2020 9:51 AM
Turn Five
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 9:51 AM
I SLASH.
Curtis Sinnoch 10/26/2020 9:51 AM
I REST
Narrator 10/26/2020 9:52 AM

2
9:52 AM
Curtis takes 12 damage.
Jules Lavelle 10/26/2020 9:53 AM
That's the way, Sinnoch! Show him your chin!
Narrator 10/26/2020 9:53 AM
Turn Six
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 9:53 AM
I REST.
Curtis Sinnoch 10/26/2020 9:53 AM

Narrator 10/26/2020 9:53 AM
yes
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 9:54 AM

Narrator 10/26/2020 9:54 AM
yep
Curtis Sinnoch 10/26/2020 9:54 AM

9:58 AM
Turn Seven
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 9:58 AM
I Lunge!
Curtis Sinnoch 10/26/2020 9:59 AM
I REST
Narrator 10/26/2020 10:00 AM
Lucien attempts to turn the tide, suddenly pivoting. Though he can barely stand, the doves flutter around him, as though guiding his blade into Curtis's unprotected flank.
10:00 AM
Curtis takes 24 damage.
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 10:00 AM
I SURRENDER!
2
Narrator 10/26/2020 10:00 AM
Curtis is the winner!
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 10:00 AM
(Just to note, I was down to 30 hp haha)
Narrator 10/26/2020 10:01 AM
Curtis, gain 1 base Expertise and no status, as Lucien is not in a regiment.
Curtis Sinnoch 10/26/2020 10:01 AM
Curtis stands, swaying slightly, but having taken less damage then the single blow to Lucien's shoulder
10:02 AM
...Pathetic. You slash a man, then immediately surrender. You made the right choice - leave with your tail between your legs, while you still stand
10:02 AM
(OOC - the next hit would have killed you!)
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 10:02 AM
(agreed!)
Curtis Sinnoch 10/26/2020 10:03 AM
(Good call!)
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 10:03 AM
(I submitted all Blocks and a Rest with the intent to surrender)
Jules Lavelle 10/26/2020 10:03 AM
R is not rest. R is Riposte. I adjusted that for you though
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 10:03 AM
haha thanks!
10:03 AM
X?
Jules Lavelle 10/26/2020 10:03 AM
yeah
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 10:03 AM
gotcha
Curtis Sinnoch 10/26/2020 10:03 AM
(Gotta take those mandatory rests! Yeah... I realized the Cut was brutal)
Ghôst Touchard 10/26/2020 10:04 AM
(The cut was half my total hp)
Curtis Sinnoch 11/15/2020 2:07 PM
@Jules Lavelle - Stand and Deliver! I have been practicing with my daggers! Though... this is a disagreement and a demand for satisfaction. I shall be using my Sabre
harry 11/15/2020 3:18 PM
wooo, let's see some fluids!

Narrator 11/19/2020 2:20 PM
The gentlemen step out into the street, the entire regiment looking on. A duel that sheds noble blood is no laughing matter. @Curtis Sinnoch's eyes blaze with righteous indignation. @Jules Lavelle seems resigned, but not afraid.
2:21 PM
Each man wields his sabre, not yet having adopted the regiment's own weapon as their preferred killing implement.
Jules Lavelle 11/19/2020 2:23 PM
If I don't survive, ask Lady Landry to visit my grave every full moon.
Curtis Sinnoch 11/19/2020 2:25 PM
If you don't survive, it is your own doing - I have no intention on killing you, @Jules Lavelle, merely to teach a lesson
Jules Lavelle 11/19/2020 2:25 PM
This duel is a lesson in your fragile ego, and nothing more. En Garde!
Curtis Sinnoch 11/19/2020 2:26 PM
Have at you!
Narrator 11/19/2020 2:26 PM

Narrator 11/19/2020 2:30 PM
Curtis continues his assault, dealing 30 damage to Jules. The Royal Foot Guards politely applaud.
Jules Lavelle 11/19/2020 2:30 PM
Such brazen insolence.
Curtis Sinnoch 11/19/2020 2:33 PM
And with first blood, I know my fury was righteous! I've claimed my blood, sir, and know it was just. If you stand down, I shall follow suit
Jules Lavelle 11/19/2020 2:34 PM
So speaks the coward!
Narrator 11/19/2020 2:34 PM
Round Seven
Jules Lavelle 11/19/2020 2:34 PM
I rest
Curtis Sinnoch 11/19/2020 2:34 PM

Curtis Sinnoch 11/19/2020 2:35 PM
I CUT
Jules Lavelle 11/19/2020 2:35 PM
I rest
Narrator 11/19/2020 2:36 PM
Jules fumbles with his blade, his blood-slicked hands unable to grip properly. Curtis seizes the advantage, cutting his thigh to the quick. Jules drops to one knee, barely supporting his weight, using his blade as a cane.
2:36 PM
Round Ten
Jules Lavelle 11/19/2020 2:36 PM
I surrender
Curtis Sinnoch 11/19/2020 2:38 PM
Curtis wipes his blade clean, sheathes it, and mentions to the Foot Guard to assist Jules
2:38 PM
I wish for no more blood of yours, sir - my thirst, and that of my blade, are both Slaked. Where shall we take you to get patched up?
Jules Lavelle 11/19/2020 2:39 PM
Away with you! To attack a regimental fellow over a difference in military strategy? I'll have no more of your company, sir.
Narrator 11/19/2020 2:40 PM
Curtis gains one expertise and loses 1 status point for beating a friend.
Curtis Sinnoch 11/19/2020 2:40 PM
I believe you and I have quite different definitions of "military strategy" and "outright insult" - I shall give you the peace you request. Good Day.

Narrator 2/14/2021 2:11 PM
With his free hand, Curtis grabs Jules's collar and pulls down, bringing his knee up to meet his opponent's descending chin. The sound of broken ivory can be heard clear across the yard, and shards of shattered teeth spill in a bloody stream from Jules' slack jaw. Curtis releases Jules, who slumps to the ground, unconscious.
3
4
Jules Lavelle 2/14/2021 2:12 PM
You...win....sir....
Curtis Sinnoch 2/14/2021 2:12 PM
Ah! Good Sir, my apologies! I appear to have over-done myself!
2:13 PM
Curtis snaps his fingers, urging a pair of attendants to hustle over and assist Jules

Narrator 6/28/2021 8:27 PM
On the 2nd week of January, still reeling from losing his love, @laiv.wire drowns his sorrows at Red Philips. Who should walk in but his newfound nemesis, @Abelard Soucy?
1
1
1
1
Abelard Soucy 6/28/2021 8:31 PM
Oh, @laiv.wire , how nice to see you...errr, I don't suppose we could put this off to another week. I seemed to have pulled a muscle in my groin, you see ..
1
1
Abelard Soucy 7/1/2021 2:51 PM
( @laiv.wire I'll be using a rapier)
Ghôst Touchard 7/1/2021 2:52 PM
those are words I haven't heard in some time!
laiv.wire 7/1/2021 2:53 PM
(Sabre)
Narrator 7/2/2021 9:18 PM
The owner of Red Phillips chuckles. "Mind the furniture, gents!" The chairs and tables have clearly been broken and reassembled many times already.
9:19 PM
@Abelard Soucy has the advantage. @laiv.wire will submit his first twelve turns' worth of actions. @Abelard Soucy will submit six.
9:19 PM
Please confirm that your respective Expertises with your chosen weapons are at 10 and 4.
laiv.wire 7/2/2021 9:20 PM
Acknowledged
Abelard Soucy 7/2/2021 9:23 PM
Actually at 11

Due to the vast difference in skill, @laiv.wire must add 3 extra Rest actions into every 12-turn sequence.

Narrator 7/2/2021 9:38 PM
Guerinet leaps out of the way of Henri's powerful reverse slash. In his place, a chair is completely destroyed, never to be repaired. The owner is too enthralled by the action to notice.
9:39 PM
Round Six

Narrator 7/27/2021 11:50 AM
@Joseph Bousain I will now explain the duel process.
1
11:51 AM
this is the form you will use to submit your actions: https://forms.gle/8F5DDx6ZPV4HFjhR6
Dueling Turn Submission
11:51 AM
I will ask you to call them out as we go round by round. the form is so I can review beforehand and make sure there's no illegal actions
11:51 AM
have you reviewed the dueling tables?
Joseph Bousain 7/27/2021 11:51 AM
Yup!
Narrator 7/27/2021 11:52 AM
great. to be clear, please submit your orders in terms of Routines. so you can indicate "Lunge, Jump Back, Block, Slash", for example
1
Joseph Bousain 7/27/2021 11:53 AM
If something is in brackets, does that mean it's optional?
Narrator 7/27/2021 11:53 AM
no, just that there's a special rule affecting it
11:53 AM
the Slash routine can "inherit" an ending rest from the routine before
11:54 AM
but if that routine has no ending rest, the slash needs two full rests
11:54 AM
so a starting slash would be X - X - S
11:54 AM
but a slash after a lunge would look like this: X - L - X - X - S
11:55 AM
for Parry, if you manage to perform the P action when your enemy performs an L, you get do to the R (Riposte) the next round
11:55 AM
if the enemy does anything but L, the (R) becomes a rest
11:57 AM
@Joseph Bousain, you have the advantage in the duel, so you will only submit 6 turns' worth of routines your first submission. @Abelard Soucy will submit 12
11:57 AM
you can go over if you want, it just means you'll give up some of your advantage by not being able to react to your enemy's orders as quickly
11:58 AM
have you decided on a weapon? note that different weapons have different damage modifiers for various attack types
Joseph Bousain 7/27/2021 11:59 AM
I summon my manservant to look over my weapons and select my trusty rapier (edited)
Narrator 7/27/2021 12:00 PM
@Abelard Soucy , your weapon?
Abelard Soucy 7/27/2021 12:02 PM
also the rapier
Narrator 7/27/2021 12:07 PM

12:07 PM
I will declare each round. Then the duelists declare their action for that round. Then I announce the result.

Narrator 7/27/2021 12:28 PM
Clarification for optional routines -- they can only be performed in the middle of another routine if the only actions performed in that routine so far have been rests

Joseph Bousain 10/18/2021 2:26 PM
Upon strolling through the streets of Paris one morn, Louis spots his beloved shopping with @Blastagarded. Naturally such an insult to his name cannot stand and Louis challenges the chap to a duel in the 1st week of this month
Joseph Bousain
Upon strolling through the streets of Paris one morn, Louis spots his beloved shopping with @Blastagarded. Naturally such an insult to his name cannot stand and Louis challenges the chap to a duel in the 1st week of this month
Blastagarded 10/18/2021 2:38 PM
Initially worried about the consequences of such a duel, Jean sighs relief when his neural link kicks in: "Threat assessment: Limited - Temporal Relevance: Negligible" I believe the lady is free to make whatever choices she desires. So this all seems very foolish and is unlikely to change anything. However, if you insist that me hurting you will make you feel better, I can oblige and add you to my itinerary for the first week of the month. (edited)
Blastagarded 10/18/2021 3:59 PM
Shall we meet at Red Phillips that week?
Joseph Bousain 10/18/2021 4:00 PM
Very well sir
Blastagarded 10/18/2021 4:03 PM
Jean's neural link switches to calendar mode and adds the entry: Give a beatdown to some rando
Narrator 10/23/2021 5:24 PM
are the duelists each at 8 EXP with their weapon of choice?
Joseph Bousain 10/23/2021 5:25 PM
I definitely am
Blastagarded 10/23/2021 5:25 PM

Narrator 10/23/2021 5:27 PM

Joseph Bousain 10/23/2021 5:28 PM
Louis draws his rapier and makes his way through the crowd
Blastagarded 10/23/2021 5:30 PM
Jean leisurely gets up and stretches, activating his implanted sobriety meds and adjusting his hormone balance for combat. To the outside observer, the man can clearly drink like a fish. He draws his sabre and moves into position for the duel.

5:31 PM
Again my friend, this seems kind of foolish. The lady clearly made her choice.
Joseph Bousain 10/23/2021 5:31 PM
No doubt she shall see through your tricks soon enough
